citation_short: UN Commission of Inquiry on Libya (2012)
title: Report of the International Commission of Inquiry on Libya, A/HRC/19/68
author_or_org: United Nations Human Rights Council / Commission of Inquiry on Libya
geographic_scope: Libya national, with Misrata and Sirte sections
strengths: Authoritative conflict-wide investigation; strong for theater description, side attribution, and legal characterization of attack patterns.
limitations: Not a city-by-city fatality ledger; civilian casualty totals are partial and not designed as full mortality series.
notes: Key geometry source for Misrata (trapped civilians, siege timing) and Sirte (indiscriminate thuwar attacks). Also records limited documented NATO civilian-casualty incidents.

title: After the Fall: Libya's Evolving Armed Groups (Working Paper 12)
date_scope: 2011 fighting, based on 2011 field interviews and registration records
strengths: Best source found for Misrata fighter growth over time; explicitly distinguishes fighters from support/logistics personnel; grounded in Misrata fieldwork.
limitations: Figure 4 is graphical rather than tabular, so person-months require visual interpolation; does not provide loyalist attacker strength.
notes: Core denominator source for Misrata anti-Qadhafi fighter exposure.

title: Libya: Government Attacks in Misrata Kill Civilians
strengths: Contemporaneous medical-source reporting from inside the siege; explicit count for killed and wounded/hospitalized.
limitations: HRW says it could not independently verify the figures and could not determine the extent of government versus rebel responsibility for civilian casualties.
notes: Useful mixed-status anchor; not sufficient alone for a civilian/combatant split.

title: Snipers strike fear into civilians in Libya's Misrata
strengths: Provides a mixed total and an explicitly identified civilian sub-count from a casualty-tracking medical committee.
limitations: Civilian figure is likely an identified minimum rather than a complete civilian count; still not a named-victim registry.
notes: Important disagreement source because its 85 civilians is lower than other 'majority civilian' hospital/morgue formulations.

title: Misrata and Sirte, March-October 2011
author_or_org: Action on Armed Violence / International Network on Explosive Weapons
strengths: Mechanism-specific civilian harm reporting for explosive violence; good for contextualizing civilian exposure and undercount risk.
limitations: EVMP civilian casualties combine deaths and injuries; some totals are mechanism-specific or area-broader than the core episode; not a full all-cause death registry.
notes: Useful auxiliary source; should not be converted mechanically into civilian deaths.

title: Libya: Misratah - Under Siege and Under Fire
pages_or_sections: Main text; casualty and population sections
strengths: Strong qualitative detail on civilian harm, siege conditions, and internal displacement; gives a contemporaneous population baseline.
limitations: Not a complete mortality ledger; some casualty phrasing is qualitative ('scores', 'hundreds').
notes: Important for lower-bound civilian death anchor and for the judgment that civilians largely remained inside the city even when moving internally.

title: Libyan armed conflict 2011: Mortality, injury and population displacement
author_or_org: Mohamed A. Daw, Abdallah El-Bouzedi, Aghnaya A. Dau
strengths: Broad conflict-wide provincial mortality and displacement overview; useful for identifying where violence concentrated.
limitations: Province-level and year-long; mixes soldiers and civilians; not suitable as a direct city-episode numerator or denominator without further decomposition.
notes: Used as contextual validation, not as the main Misrata-city numerator source.

title: Contemporaneous population reporting on Misrata
strengths: Contemporaneous reporting brackets Misrata's exposed civilian stock between approximately 300,000 and 400,000.
limitations: Journalistic rather than census/admin data; exact city boundary for the population figure is not specified.
notes: Used only as low/high population bracket, not as a precise census.

title: Misrata battlefield death dispatches
strengths: Provides explicit, side-specific anti-Qadhafi fighter death counts inside the episode window.
limitations: Event-based and incomplete; not a cumulative series.
notes: Used only as lower-bound evidence for defender combatant deaths.

title: More than 400 killed, 2,000 hurt in Tripoli - report
strengths: Useful early mixed death/injury headline for Tripoli offensive.
limitations: No status split, side split, or exposure denominator.
notes: Sufficient to justify Tripoli as a separate episode, not sufficient for a core DR estimate.

title: Gaddafi hometown a hazardous prize for Libya's NTC
strengths: Provides a side-specific rebel loss count and a pre-battle city population headline.
limitations: Only one front and one date slice; no full-city civilian death series.
notes: Useful for episodeization and open problems, not yet enough for a Sirte DR.

title: Unacknowledged Deaths: Civilian Casualties in NATO's Air Campaign in Libya
geographic_scope: Multiple Libyan strike sites including Sirte-related incidents
strengths: Best focused source found for side-attributed NATO-inflicted civilian deaths and subgroup breakdowns.
limitations: Covers eight investigated strikes, not a full episode-wide Sirte ledger.
notes: Critical for side-attribution retrofit even though not used in the core Misrata estimate.
